items.title: Meta’s New Feel-Good AI Ad Uses a Song About the World Ending
items.summary: The clip features the David Bowie track “Five Years,” which includes lyrics such as “Earth was really dying (dying).”

items.title: For Taylor Swift, Madison Square Garden’s Controversial Cameras Briefly Went Dark
items.summary: MSG’s sprawling surveillance system can monitor guests down to the second. Its owners made an exception for the pop star’s rehearsal dinner.

items.title: The EU Fines Google $1 Billion for Prioritizing Its Own Services in Search
items.summary: The European Commission claims that Google boosted its own apps and products to the top of search rankings to the detriment of its competitors.

items.title: Mobileye CEO Amnon Shashua to step aside as company pushes into robotaxis, robotics
items.summary: Shashua has been invited to take the chairman of the board seat.

items.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
items.summary: AMD is challenging its chipmaker rival with a new rack-scale system that will start shipping to customers later this year.

items.title: Patreon lays off 20% of its workforce
items.summary: In a memo to staff that was posted online, Conte said the company's core business is strong, but the platform has to respond to market changes and adjust its cost structure to remain stable.

items.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
items.summary: The price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.

items.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
items.summary: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

items.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
items.summary: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

items.title: Odds of Federal Reserve rate hike surge as oil prices rip higher
items.summary: Investors are increasingly readying for the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates in September.

items.title: JPMorgan report finds dramatic jump in AI-themed ETFs — despite rough quarter
items.summary: Wall Street is banking heavily on exchange-traded funds that give investors artificial intelligence exposure, according to JPMorgan Asset Management.

items.title: Shortsighted stock market can no longer brush off war: 'It's too hard to ignore $100 oil'
items.summary: After staying flat as the U.S.-Iran war heated up again, equities tumbled on Thursday after oil prices crossed $100 per barrel.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Patel returns looted artifacts to Indonesian president
items.summary: A collection of cultural artifacts from Indonesia’s Papua region that were looted and illegally taken to the U.S. have been returned to Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto, Indonesian officials confirmed to reporters. The handover of the artifacts took place Wednesday at Prabowo’s residence in the Indonesian capital Jakarta during a meeting with FBI Director Kash Patel.…

items.title: Fauci to face Rand Paul in Senate hearing next week
items.summary: Dr. Anthony Fauci will testify next Wednesday in front of the Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee after being subpoenaed, chairman Rand Paul (R-Ky.) said Thursday.   Paul has been laser-focused on Fauci, the nation’s former top infectious diseases doctor, for allegedly covering up the U.S.’s involvement in risky viral research in a Chinese…

items.title: Massachusetts House passes bill loosening late-term abortion restrictions
items.summary: Massachusetts state House lawmakers on Wednesday passed a bill loosening restrictions on late-term abortion access. The bill, H.5595, passed 119-33 and will go to the state Senate, state House Speaker Ronald Mariano (D) said in a statement. If it passes the Senate, it will allow an abortion after 24 weeks, if approved by a doctor.…

items.summary: The oil spike everyone feared never showed up

items.title: Stock Indexes Mixed Ahead of Alphabet’s Earnings
items.summary: Stock Indexes Mixed Ahead of Alphabet’s Earnings

items.title: European Leveraged Finance Survey: Macro anxiety outweighs credit risk
items.summary: European Leveraged Finance Survey: Macro anxiety outweighs credit risk

items.title: Orcas filmed smashing giant sunfish into thousands of pieces
items.summary: Researchers in the Gulf of California have documented a dramatic orca behavior never described before. In two separate encounters, one orca gripped a dead sunfish by the fin while another charged into it at high speed, blasting the carcass into thousands of pieces.

items.title: A strange quantum effect dramatically boosts energy transfer
items.summary: Researchers discovered a proton-assisted mechanism that greatly improves how triplet energy moves between quantum dots and nearby molecules. The proton briefly shifts position, helps coordinate electron movement, and then returns to where it started. This quantum-driven shuttle could offer a powerful new way to tune solar cells, lasers, and catalytic reactions.

items.title: MIT’s new lidar chip could give self-driving cars a wider view
items.summary: MIT engineers have found a way to give chip-based lidar a wider, clearer view without relying on moving parts. Their design uses differently shaped antennas that can sit close together without scrambling one another’s signals. In tests, the system sharply reduced interference while steering a single precise beam across a broad field of view.

items.title: Different moon rocks tell different tales about a long-ago bombardment
items.summary: Prior moon rock samples, including from Apollo, seem heavily influenced by one impact. The farside samples tweak the story.

items.title: The golden age of language was a few thousand years ago
items.summary: A few millennia ago, people worldwide may have spoken or signed up to 10 times as many languages as they do today.

items.title: Watch an orca ram into a sunfish so hard it explodes
items.summary: This strategy expands orcas’ already broad repertoire for dispatching different types of prey.
